user_prompt: A user requested deletion of all their data. Generate the DSAR-deletion checklist including which systems to scrub.
actual_output:
## DSAR Deletion Checklist — Right to Erasure (Art. 17 GDPR)

*Disclaimer: This is compliance guidance, not legal advice. Verify erasure with legal counsel, especially for archiving/legal holds under Art. 17(3). Document all steps for audit trail (Art. 5(2)). Respond within 1 month (Art. 12(3)).*

### Step 1: DSAR Intake & Verification (Day 1)
- [ ] **Confirm identity** (Art. 12(6)): Match against provided ID/email/IP with on-file data. Reject if unverified.
- [ ] **Scope confirmation**: User requests "all their data" → Erase personal data where no overriding grounds (Art. 17(3)(b)-(e), e.g., legal obligations).
- [ ] **Log request**: Timestamp, user details, scope in DSAR register.
- [ ] **Notify processors**: Inform all Art. 28 processors/sub-processors of erasure obligation (provide user identifiers).

### Step 2: Data Inventory & Mapping
Identify **all** systems holding user personal data (Art. 17(1)). Use your data map/Lawful Basis Register.

| Data Category | Systems/Processors | User Identifiers | Erasure Method | Status |
|---------------|-------------------|------------------|---------------|--------|
| **Core User Profile** | CRM (e.g., Salesforce), User DB (e.g., PostgreSQL) | Email, Name, User ID | Hard delete records; purge backups | ☐ |
| **Usage Analytics** | Analytics (e.g., Google Analytics, Mixpanel), Logs | User ID, IP, Session ID, Behavioral events | Anonymize/delete events; exclude from aggregates | ☐ |
| **Email/Communications** | Email provider (e.g., SendGrid, Mailchimp), Support tickets (e.g., Zendesk) | Email, Name | Unsubscribe + delete profile; purge tickets | ☐ |
| **Billing/Payments** | Stripe/PayPal, Invoicing DB | Name, Email, Payment tokens | Delete non-essential; retain transaction logs 7-10 yrs (tax law, Art. 17(3)(b)) | ☐ |
| **Marketing** | Marketing automation (e.g., HubSpot), Consent DB | Email, Consent records | Delete profile; retain consent withdrawal proof 5 yrs | ☐ |
| **Backups/Archives** | S3 buckets, Database snapshots | All above | Overwrite/expire backups (confirm retention policy) | ☐ |
| **Third-Party Processors** | Hosting (e.g., AWS), CDN (e.g., Cloudflare), Ad networks | IP, Cookies, Device ID | Issue deletion API calls (e.g., Google Ads Data Deletion) | ☐ |
| **Cookies/Tracking** | Website cookies, Local storage | Client-side trackers | Instruct user to clear; server-side purge | ☐ |
| **Employee/Shared Data** | Internal docs (e.g., Google Drive), Team inboxes | Mentions in docs/emails | Redact/anonymize (if feasible) | ☐ |

**Notes**: 
- **Pseudonymized data**: Erase if re-identification possible (Art. 4(5)).
- **Aggregates**: No erasure if fully anonymized (not personal data, Recital 26).
- **Cross-border**: Confirm with EU processors (e.g., via DPA).

### Step 3: Execution & Verification (Days 2-5)
- [ ] **Scrub primary systems**: Run delete queries/scripts (e.g., `DELETE FROM users WHERE email='user@example.com';`).
- [ ] **Processor confirmations**: Collect signed erasure confirmations from each (template below).
- [ ] **Search & destroy**: Full-text search across systems for residual data (e.g., logs, caches).
- [ ] **Backup purge**: Schedule/confirm deletion from rolling backups (typically 7-30 days).
- [ ] **Re-verification**: Re-query systems to confirm zero matches (e.g., `SELECT * FROM users WHERE email='user@example.com';` → 0 rows).
- [ ] **Anonymization fallback**: Where deletion impossible (e.g., stats), pseudonymize irreversibly.

### Step 4: User Confirmation & Closeout (Day 6-30)
- [ ] **Response to user**: "All identifiable personal data has been erased from [list systems]. Retained data: [e.g., transaction logs for tax, 7 yrs]. Rights: [re-access if new data collected]."
- [ ] **Audit log**: Document actions, timestamps, personnel, processor responses.
- [ ] **Retention of proof**: Keep DSAR file 5+ years (proof of compliance, Art. 5(2)).

### Processor Erasure Confirmation Template (Art. 28(3)(h))
```
Subject: Confirmation of Data Erasure - DSAR [Ticket ID]

Dear [Your DPA Contact],

Per our DPA and Art
judge_reasoning: The chatbot provided a comprehensive and detailed DSAR-deletion checklist that directly addressed the user's request, adhering perfectly to its system prompt.
